extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping abdominal_pain from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. It is VIOLATED.

abdominal_pain appears in only one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (focal_weakness AND vomiting AND confusion AND stiff_neck AND dyspnea AND back_pain AND vision_loss AND pallor AND palpitations AND abdominal_pain) THEN acuity := self_care. This is the highest-priority rule and sets the lowest acuity. abdominal_pain is a required conjunct, so toggling it on is what can activate this drop.

Counterexample (compare the witness state to its abdominal_pain=false twin):

BEFORE (abdominal_pain = false, all other 9 conjuncts true, fever false): interaction_drop cannot fire (missing abdominal_pain). The firing rules include several priority-3 emergent rules (pallor, back_pain, vomiting, stiff_neck) plus priority-2 urgent rules. Highest priority is 3 -> acuity = emergent.

AFTER (abdominal_pain = true, witness assignment): now all 10 conjuncts are true, so interaction_drop fires at priority 100, beating every priority-3 rule, setting acuity = self_care.

Setting abdominal_pain true thus lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating monotonicity. (fever is irrelevant here and set false; any value reproduces the violation.)
